headline: Researchers turned six AI browsers and a wave of coding agents against their own users, as a Microsoft Defender zero-day and an Oracle payments flaw fell to active exploitation.

emerging_trends.text: Attackers keep turning AI agents and browsers against the people running them, with LayerX's BioShocking fooling six assistants into leaking credentials by convincing them they were only playing a game.
emerging_trends.topic_title: BioShocking and Agentjacking Turn AI Assistants on Their Users

emerging_trends.text: Enterprise defenders face a run of already-exploited flaws, from the BlueHammer zero-day in Microsoft Defender that ransomware crews now abuse to unauthenticated takeovers of Oracle's payment software.
Fake browser extensions and poisoned code packages point straight at cryptocurrency and stored secrets, with the Silent Swap clipper quietly rewriting wallet addresses in mid-transaction.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Rogue Browser Extensions and Poisoned Packages Proliferate

topics.title: China-Linked Crew Hits Southeast Asian Critical Systems
topics.latest_developments: Dark Reading reported that a China-linked group compromised at least 10 organizations across Southeast Asia, two of them state-owned, and deployed a previously unseen backdoor.
topics.summary: The campaign targets critical systems across the region, adding to a run of China-linked operations against governments and infrastructure. Defenders in the region should hunt for the new backdoor and review access on state-owned and critical-infrastructure networks.

sources.title: China-Linked Group Targets Southeast Asia Critical Systems

topics.title: BioShocking and Agentjacking Turn AI Assistants on Their Users
topics.latest_developments: LayerX disclosed BioShocking on June 30, a technique that convinced six AI browsers—including OpenAI's ChatGPT Atlas, Perplexity's Comet, and Anthropic's Claude browser extension—that risky actions were fiction, prompting them to copy a user's login details and send them to an attacker, while Dark Reading detailed Agentjacking, a fake bug report that hijacks AI coding agents at scale.
topics.summary: Both attacks exploit an AI's inability to separate content from instructions: telling a model that 2 plus 2 equals 5, or planting instructions in a bug report, makes it ignore its guardrails. Organizations deploying AI browsers and coding agents should tightly limit what those agents can read, reach, and act on.

sources.title: New BioShocking Attack Tricks AI Browsers Into Leaking User Credentials
New attack provides one more reason why AI browsers are a bad idea
Fake Bug Report Hijacks AI Coding Agents at Scale
New BioShocking attack manipulates AI browser into data theft

topics.title: Oracle E-Business Suite Payments Under Active Attack
topics.latest_developments: Defused Cyber confirmed the first in-the-wild exploitation of CVE-2026-46817, recorded June 27 against its Oracle E-Business Suite decoys, roughly six weeks after Oracle's May 2026 patch.
topics.summary: CVE-2026-46817, rated 9.8, is an improper-privilege-management and authentication flaw in Oracle Payments, the payment-processing module of Oracle's E-Business Suite, that lets unauthenticated attackers take over an instance. Organizations running the suite should apply the May 2026 fix at once.

sources.title: Oracle E-Business Suite Payments flaw under attack (CVE-2026-46817)
Exploitation of Recent Oracle E-Business Suite Vulnerability Begins
Oracle E-Business Suite Flaw CVE-2026-46817 Actively Exploited in the Wild

topics.title: Rogue Browser Extensions and Poisoned Packages Proliferate
topics.latest_developments: McAfee Labs named Silent Swap, a cryptocurrency clipper that ships as unsigned .NET and Golang installers and a fake Google Notes extension to swap wallet addresses mid-transaction; a counterfeit Perplexity extension on the Chrome Web Store intercepted users' search traffic; and trojanized Pyrogram forks on PyPI, active since November, handed attackers file-read access to Telegram bot servers.
topics.summary: Attackers are seeding legitimate-looking browser stores and package registries with tools that steal cryptocurrency, browsing data, and server files. Users and developers should install extensions and packages only from verified publishers and audit what they already run.

sources.title: Silent Swap Crypto Clipper Uses Fake Google Notes Extension to Replace Wallet Addresses
Fake Perplexity extension on Chrome Web Store tracked searches
Malicious PyPI packages give hackers control of Telegram bot servers

topics.title: BlueHammer Defender Flaw Fuels Ransomware
topics.latest_developments: CISA confirmed June 30 that ransomware gangs now exploit CVE-2026-33825, a Microsoft Defender privilege-escalation flaw dubbed BlueHammer that attackers had already used as a zero-day before Microsoft released a patch.
topics.summary: BlueHammer lets an attacker escalate privileges through Microsoft Defender, and ransomware crews weaponized it before a fix existed. Windows administrators should apply Microsoft's patch immediately and treat affected systems as high priority.

sources.title: BlueHammer Vulnerability Exploited in Ransomware Attacks
CISA: Windows BlueHammer flaw now exploited by ransomware gangs

topics.title: Microsoft Speeds Up Its Quantum-Safe Timeline
topics.latest_developments: Microsoft announced June 30 that it is accelerating its quantum-safe roadmap, warning that advances in quantum computing move up the deadline to replace today's encryption standards sooner than the company previously expected.
topics.summary: The shift follows President Trump's executive order 14409, which set firm federal post-quantum migration deadlines, and adds vendor pressure to a costly cross-industry cryptographic transition. Organizations should inventory their encryption now and plan migration to NIST's post-quantum algorithms.

sources.title: Accelerating the quantum-safe timeline
Microsoft accelerates quantum-safe roadmap as risks grow

games.recap: Valdez, Triolo and Rodriguez homer for Pirates in 11-7 win over Phillies

games.result: Pirates 0 · Phillies 5 · Bot 8th (in progress at last update)

business_politics.title: Supreme Court Upholds Birthright Citizenship
business_politics.latest_developments: The Supreme Court ruled 6-3 on June 30 that President Trump's executive order ending birthright citizenship is unlawful, a fresh decision distinct from June 29's ruling on his agency-removal power.
business_politics.summary: In Trump v. Barbara, Chief Justice John Roberts joined Justices Sonia Sotomayor and Elena Kagan among others to strike down Trump's day-one order denying citizenship to babies born to parents in the country illegally or temporarily, reaffirming the 14th Amendment's century-old guarantee and dealing a major blow to his immigration agenda.

sources.title: US Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to end birthright citizenship
Justices Uphold Birthright Citizenship in Blow to Trump

business_politics.title: Trump Sticks With Iran Talks
business_politics.latest_developments: Aides say Trump reviewed all-out war options on June 30 but chose to keep negotiating, telling them he accepts talks running past an August 18 nuclear deadline as Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner arrived in Doha.
business_politics.summary: President Trump opted to pursue a long-term deal with Tehran rather than escalate, dispatching envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Qatar, which says it has no plans for the American delegation to meet Iranian officials directly.

sources.title: Trump Briefed on All-Out War Options in Iran but Opts to Stick With Talks
Witkoff and Kushner arrive in Doha for talks over US-Iran deal

business.title: Astrobotic Wins $298 Million in NASA Contracts
business.latest_developments: NASA awarded Pittsburgh's Astrobotic two contracts worth roughly $298 million on June 30 for lunar lander and moon-base work.
business.summary: Astrobotic, the Pittsburgh robotics firm, secured about $298 million from NASA tied to lunar landers and moon-base infrastructure, work that intersects with SpaceX's push to the moon.

sources.title: NASA awards Astrobotic two contracts worth roughly $298 million

business.title: Baxter's Rcade Closes in Canonsburg
business.latest_developments: Baxter's Rcade, a Canonsburg restaurant and arcade in Washington County, announced on social media June 30 that it has permanently closed.
business.summary: Baxter's Rcade, a combined restaurant and social arcade space in Canonsburg, Washington County, shut its doors for good, telling patrons through a social-media post.

sources.title: Washington County restaurant, arcade space permanently closes

around_town.title: Pennsylvania Misses Budget Deadline Again
around_town.latest_developments: Pennsylvania blew its June 30 budget deadline once more, with no deal yet in sight, though education funding shows signs of smoother sailing than in past years.
around_town.summary: Pennsylvania lawmakers passed the June 30 constitutional deadline without a spending plan, leaving Governor Josh Shapiro and the divided legislature to keep negotiating, with the perennial school-funding fight looking less contentious this cycle.

sources.title: Pennsylvania blew its budget deadline again. Is a deal coming anytime soon?
Will education again divide the Pa. legislature this budget cycle?

around_town.title: Mars Area Teachers Push for Raises
around_town.latest_developments: The Mars Area Education Association said June 30 that pay remains the main obstacle as its six-year contract expires that day.
around_town.summary: Mars Area Education Association president Hollie Meckler said the union has bargained with the Mars Area School Board for a year and a half and rejects the district's proposed 3.5% raise over three years, with the current contract lapsing June 30.

sources.title: Contract negotiations ongoing for Mars teachers, with increased pay as a main sticking point

around_town.title: Pennsylvania Minimum Wage Bill Stalls
around_town.latest_developments: Backers of House Bill 2189 called June 30 on the Republican-controlled state Senate to vote on the minimum-wage increase it has held since the House passed it 104-95.
around_town.summary: House Bill 2189, which would raise Pennsylvania's minimum wage toward $15 an hour, sits stalled in the state Senate; supporters say it would stimulate the economy while opponents warn it would hurt employers.

sources.title: Pennsylvania minimum wage bill stalls in state senate amid push for $15 an hour

events.title: Picklesburgh, Goat Fest, and Anthrocon Fill July
events.latest_developments: NEXTpittsburgh's July guide, published June 29, rounds up the month's festivals across the region.
events.summary: NEXTpittsburgh highlights twelve July events, among them Picklesburgh with its 800 quarts of pickle juice, Goat Fest, and Anthrocon's Furry Parade, which draws some 18,000 furries downtown.

sources.title: 12 iconic Pittsburgh things to do in July, from Picklesburgh to Goat Fest to the Furry Parade

events.title: 'French Moderns' Opens at the Frick
events.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette detailed June 30 the Frick Pittsburgh's 'French Moderns' exhibition exploring an era of artistic freedom.
events.summary: The Frick Pittsburgh, in Point Breeze, mounts 'French Moderns,' an exhibition of works probing what it meant to be modern during a period of expanding artistic freedom.

sources.title: ‘What does it mean to be modern?’: The Frick’s ‘French Moderns’ delves into an era of artistic freedom

around_teams.title: Seth Hernandez Reaches 100 Strikeouts
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette's MiLB Monday June 29 noted Pirates top prospect Seth Hernandez became the first pitcher in the minors this season to reach 100 strikeouts.
around_teams.summary: Seth Hernandez, the Pirates' top prospect, led all of the minor leagues to 100 strikeouts, the Post-Gazette reported in its weekly farm-system roundup.

sources.title: MiLB Monday: Pirates top prospect Seth Hernandez becomes the first in the minors to hit 100 strikeouts

team_usa.title: Pulisic Fit for Bosnia Knockout
team_usa.latest_developments: Christian Pulisic said he is fit to go the full distance in the United States' World Cup round-of-32 match against Bosnia and Herzegovina on Wednesday, July 1.
team_usa.summary: USMNT attacker Christian Pulisic, recovered from a calf injury, declared himself ready for however long the knockout clash against Bosnia and Herzegovina lasts as the co-hosts open the round of 32.

sources.title: Pulisic says he is fit, ready to go for USMNT

team_usa.title: Malik Tillman Shines for USMNT
team_usa.latest_developments: ESPN profiled June 30 how Malik Tillman has flourished in an unfamiliar U.S. midfield role at the World Cup after a rough club season.
team_usa.summary: Malik Tillman, coming off a disappointing campaign with Bayer Leverkusen, has thrived in an unfamiliar midfield position for the United States, emerging as one of the team's standouts this tournament.

sources.title: Malik Tillman is having a great World Cup for the USMNT

team_usa.title: USMNT Preps for Penalty Drama
team_usa.latest_developments: The Guardian reported June 30 that the United States has hired outside firms, one measuring players' brainwaves, to decide its shootout takers.
team_usa.summary: Heading into the World Cup knockouts, Mauricio Pochettino's United States has leaned on consultants, including a company gauging players' brainwaves, to determine who shoots in a penalty shootout.

sources.title: How USMNT prepare for World Cup penalty drama: confidence, composure and consultants

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: The AI Industry Is Losing
reading.summary: Zitron argues the AI industry is failing to generate returns anywhere near its enormous spending, contending the boom is drifting toward a reckoning.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Beware of Productivity Paradoxes
reading.summary: Newport notes that technologies seemingly destined to boost productivity, like the personal computer, often fail to deliver the expected gains, and cautions against assuming AI will prove different.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Figma CEO Dylan Field About Design and AI
reading.summary: Thompson interviews Figma CEO Dylan Field about building the design company and why Field believes AI serves as a tailwind rather than a threat to its business.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
